volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Captives (Section 4.3.901 - Section 5.3.989b)

sections.label: Section 4.3.901
sections.original: illic hinc abiit , mihi rem summam credidit cibariam .
sections.translation: He has gone away from here , and has entrusted to me the most important concern of catering . Immortal Gods how I shall now be slicing necks off of sides ; how vast a downfall will befall the gammon [*] ; how vast a belabouring the bacon ! How great a using-up of udders , how vast a bewailing for the brawn !

sections.label: Section 4.3.905
sections.original: quanta laniis lassitudo , quanta porcinariis .
sections.translation: How great a bestirring for the butchers , how great a preparation for the porksellers ! But if I were to enumerate the rest of the things which minister to the supply of the stomach , ‘ twould be sheer delay . Now will I go off to my government , to give laws to the bacon , and , those gammons that are hanging uncondemned , [*] to give aid to them .

sections.label: Section 4.4.909
sections.original: Diéspiter te dique , Ergasile , perdant et ventrem tuom ,
sections.translation: May Jupiter and the Deities confound you , Ergasilus , and your stomach ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.910
sections.original: parasítosque omnis , et qui posthac cenam parasitis dabit .
sections.translation: and all Parasites , and every one who henceforth shall give a dinner to Parasites . Destruction and devastation and ruin have just now entered our house . I was afraid that he would be making an attack on me , as though he had been an hungry wolf . And very dreadfully , upon my faith , was I frightened at him ; he made such a gnashing with his teeth . On his arrival , the whole larder , with the meat , he turned upside down .

sections.label: Section 4.4.915
sections.original: arrípuit gladium , praetruncavit tribus tegoribus glandia ;
sections.translation: He seized a knife , and first cut off the kernels of the neck [*] from three sides . All the pots and cups he broke , except those that held a couple of gallons [*] ; of the cook he made enquiry whether the salting pans could be set on the fire to be made hot . All the cellars in the house he has broken into , and has laid the store-closet [*] open . ( At the door .) Watch him , servants , if you please ; I ’ ll go to meet the old gentleman .

sections.label: Section 4.4.920
sections.original: dicam út sibi penum alium adornet , siquidem sese uti volet ;
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell him to get ready some provisions for his own self , if , indeed , he wishes himself to make use of any . For in this place , as this man , indeed , is managing , either there ’ s nothing already , or very soon there will be nothing .

sections.label: Section 5.1.922
sections.original: Ioví disque agó gratiás merito mágnas ,
sections.translation: To Jove and to the Deities I return with reason hearty thanks , inasmuch as they have restored you to your father , and inasmuch as they have delivered me from very many afflictions ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.925
sections.original: † quae adhuc carens dum huc fui sustentabam ,
sections.translation: which , while I was obliged to be here without you , I was enduring , and inasmuch as I see that that fellow ( pointing to STALAGMUS ) is in my power , and inasmuch as his word ( pointing to PHILOCIATES ) has been found true to me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.928
sections.original: Sátis iam dolui ex ánimo , et cura mé satis et lacrumis máceravi ,
sections.translation: Enough now have I grieved from my very soul , and enough with care and tears have I disquieted myself . Enough now have I heard of your woes , which at the harbour you told me of .

sections.label: Section 5.1.930
sections.original: hóc agamus .
sections.translation: Let us now to this business .

sections.label: Section 5.1.930b
sections.original: Quid nunc , quoniam tecum servavi fidem
sections.translation: What now , since I ’ ve kept my word with you , and have caused him to be restored back again to freedom ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.931b
sections.original: Fecisti ut tibi ,
sections.translation: Philocrates , you have acted so that I can never return you thanks enough , in the degree that you merit from myself and my son .

sections.label: Section 5.1.933b
sections.original: Immo potes ,
sections.translation: Nay , but you can , father , and you will be able , and I shall be able ; and the Divinities will give the means for you

sections.label: Section 5.1.935
sections.original: ut beneficium bene merenti nostro merito muneres ;
sections.translation: to return the kindness he merits to one who deserves so highly of us ; as , my father , you are able to do to this person who so especially deserves it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.937
sections.original: Quid opust verbis ? lingua nullast qua negem quidquid roges .
sections.translation: What need is there of words ? I have no tongue with which to deny whatever you may ask of me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.938
sections.original: Postulo abs te , ut mi illum reddas servom , quem hic reliqueram
sections.translation: I ask of you to restore to me that servant whom I left here as a surety for myself ; who has always proved more faithful to me than to himself ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.940
sections.original: pro bene factis eius ut ei pretium possim reddere .
sections.translation: in order that for his services I may be enabled to give him a reward .

sections.label: Section 5.1.941
sections.original: Quod bene fecisti referetur gratia id quod postulas ;
sections.translation: Because you have acted thus kindly , the favour shall be returned , the thing that you ask ; both that and anything else that you shall ask of me , you shall obtain . And I would not have you blame me , because in my anger I have treated him harshly .

sections.label: Section 5.1.944
sections.original: Quid fecisti ?
sections.translation: What have you done ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.944b
sections.original: In lapicidinas compeditum condidi ,
sections.translation: I confined him in fetters at the stone-quarries ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.945
sections.original: ubi rescivi mihi data esse verba .
sections.translation: when I found out that I had been imposed upon .

sections.label: Section 5.1.945b
sections.original: Vae misero mihi ,
sections.translation: Ah wretched me ! That for my safety misfortunes should have happened to that best of men .

sections.label: Section 5.1.947
sections.original: At ob eam rem mihi libellam pró eo argenti ne duis :
sections.translation: Now , on this account , you need not give me even one groat of silver [*] for him . Receive him of me without cost that he may be free .

sections.label: Section 5.1.948b
sections.original: Edepol , Hegio ,
sections.translation: On my word , Hegio , you act with kindness ; but I entreat that you will order this man to be sent for .

sections.label: Section 5.1.949b
sections.original: Licet .
sections.translation: Certainly .

sections.label: Section 5.1.950
sections.original: ubi estís vos ? ite actutum , Tyndarum huc arcessite .
sections.translation: ( To the attendants , who immediately obey .) Where are you ? Go this instant , and bring Tyndarus here . ( To PHILOPOLEMUS and PHILOCRATES .) Do you go in-doors ; in the meantime , I wish to enquire of this statue for whipping [*] , what was done with my younger son . Do you go bathe in the meantime .

sections.label: Section 5.1.953b
sections.original: Sequere hac , Philocrates , me intro .—
sections.translation: Philocrates , follow me this way in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.1.953c
sections.original: Sequor .—
sections.translation: I follow you . ( They go into the house .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.954
sections.original: Age tu illuc procede , bone vir , lepidum mancupium meum .
sections.translation: Come you , step this way , you worthy fellow , my fine slave .

sections.label: Section 5.2.955
sections.original: Quid me oportet facere , ubi tu talis vir falsum autumas ?
sections.translation: What is fitting for me to do , when you , such a man as you are , are speaking false ? I was never a handsome , or a fine , or a good person , or an honest one , nor shall I ever be ; assuredly , don ’ t you be forming any hopes that I shall be honest .

sections.label: Section 5.2.958
sections.original: Propemodum ubi loci fortunae tuae sint facile intellegis .
sections.translation: You easily understand pretty well in what situation your fortunes are . If you shall prove truth-telling , you ’ ll make your lot from bad somewhat better .

sections.label: Section 5.2.960
sections.original: recte et vera loquere , sed neque vere neque tu recte adhuc
sections.translation: Speak out , then , correctly and truthfully ; but never yet truthfully or correctly have you acted .

sections.label: Section 5.2.961b
sections.original: Quod ego fatear , credin pudeat cum autumes ?
sections.translation: Do you think that I ’ m ashamed to own it , when you affirm it ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.962
sections.original: At ego faciam ut pudeat , nam in ruborem te totum dabo .
sections.translation: But I ’ ll make you to be ashamed ; for I ’ ll cause you to be blushes all over [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.963
sections.original: Eia , credo ego imperito plagas minitaris mihi .
sections.translation: Heyday — you ’ re threatening stripes , I suppose , to me , quite unaccustomed to them ! Away with them , I beg . Tell me what you bring , that you may carry off hence what you are in want of .

sections.label: Section 5.2.965
sections.original: Satis facundu'sfacundus es . sed iam fieri dicta compendi volo .
sections.translation: Very fluent indeed . But now I wish this prating to be cut short .

sections.label: Section 5.2.966
sections.original: Vt vis fiat .
sections.translation: As you desire , so be it done .

sections.label: Section 5.2.966b
sections.original: Bene morigerus fuit puer , nunc non decet .
sections.translation: As a boy he was very obedient [*] ; now that suits him not . Let ’ s to this business ; now give your attention , and inform me upon what I ask . If you tell the truth , you ’ ll make your fortunes somewhat better .

sections.label: Section 5.2.969
sections.original: Nugae istaec sunt . non me censes scire quid dignus siem ?
sections.translation: That ’ s mere trifling . Don ’ t you think that I know what I ’ m deserving of ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.970
sections.original: At ea subterfugere potis es pauca , si non omnia .
sections.translation: Still , it is in your power to escape a small portion of it , if not the whole .

sections.label: Section 5.2.971
sections.original: Pauca effugiam , scio ; nam multa evenient , et merito meo ,
sections.translation: A small portion I shall escape , I know ; but much will befall me , and with my deserving it , because I both ran away , and stole your son and sold him .

sections.label: Section 5.2.973
sections.original: Cui homini ?
sections.translation: To what person ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.973b
sections.original: Theodoromedi in Alide Polyplusio ,
sections.translation: To Theodoromedes the Polyplusian , in Elis , for six minae .

sections.label: Section 5.2.974b
sections.original: Pro di immortales , is quidem huius est pater
sections.translation: O ye immortal Gods ! He surely is the father of this person , Philocrates .

sections.label: Section 5.2.975b
sections.original: Quin melius novi quam tu et vidi saepius .
sections.translation: Why , I know him better than yourself , and have seen him more times .

sections.label: Section 5.2.976
sections.original: Serva , Iuppiter supreme , et me et meum gnatum mihi .
sections.translation: Supreme Jove , preserve both myself and my son for me . ( He goes to the door , and calls aloud .) Philocrates , by your good Genius , I do entreat you , come out , I want you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.978
sections.original: Hegio , assum . si quid me vis , impera .
sections.translation: Hegio , here am I ; if you want anything of me , command me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.978b
sections.original: Hic gnatum meum
sections.translation: He ( pointing to STALAGMUS ) declares that he sold my son to your father , in Elis , for six minae .

sections.label: Section 5.3.980
sections.original: Quam diu id factum est ?
sections.translation: How long since did that happen ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.980b
sections.original: Hic annus incipit vicensimus .
sections.translation: This is the twentieth year , commencing from it .

sections.label: Section 5.3.981
sections.original: Falsa memorat .
sections.translation: He is speaking falsely .

sections.label: Section 5.3.981b
sections.original: Aut ego aut tu . nam tibi quadrimulum
sections.translation: Either I or you do . Why , your father gave you the little child , of four years old , to be your own slave .

sections.label: Section 5.3.983
sections.original: Quid erat ei nomen ? si vera dicis , memoradum mihi .
sections.translation: What was his name ? If you are speaking the truth , tell me that , then .

sections.label: Section 5.3.984
sections.original: Paegnium vocitatust , post vos indidistis Tyndaro .
sections.translation: Paegnium , he used to be called ; afterwards , you gave him the name of Tyndarus .

sections.label: Section 5.3.985
sections.original: Cur ego te non novi ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t I recollect you ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.985b
sections.original: Quia mos est oblivisci hominibus
sections.translation: Because it ’ s the fashion for persons to forget , and not to know him whose favour is esteemed as worth nothing .

sections.label: Section 5.3.987
sections.original: Dic mihi , isne istíc fuit , quem vendidisti meo patri ,
sections.translation: Tell me , was he the person whom you sold to my father , who was given me for my private service ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.988b
sections.original: Huius filius .
sections.translation: It was his son .

sections.label: Section 5.3.989
sections.original: Vivitne is homo ?
sections.translation: Is this person now living ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.989b
sections.original: Argentum accepi , nil curavi ceterum .
sections.translation: I received the money I cared nothing about the rest .
